qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed ASN.1 length field causes denial-of-service via infinite loop in ASN1HEX.getChildIdx
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a negative exponent handling flaw in jsrsasign's modPow function (ext/jsbn2.js) causing signature verification bypass. This finding describes an unrelated ASN.1 parsing denial-of-service vulnerability in src/asn1hex-1.1.js involving malformed length fields. The root cause, affected component, and vulnerability mechanism are completely different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JWS.verify algorithm confusion allows authentication bypass when key is passed as non-PEM string
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a negative exponent handling flaw in jsrsasign's modPow function (ext/jsbn2.js) causing signature verification bypass through incorrect modular inverse computation. This finding describes an algorithm confusion attack in JWS.verify (src/jws-3.3.js) where HMAC algorithms accept non-PEM string keys. While both involve signature verification bypass, the root causes are different: incorrect modular inverse computation vs. missing key-type validation for HMAC algorithms. The vulnerable code locations (ext/jsbn2.js vs src/jws-3.3.js) and mechanisms (modPow negative exponent vs. algorithm confusion) are distinct.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a specific flaw in jsrsasign's ext/jsbn2.js where negative exponents in modPow cause incorrect modular inverses and break signature verification. The two qualifying findings describe different vulnerabilities: (1) an ASN.1 parsing DoS in asn1hex-1.1.js via malformed length fields, and (2) a JWS algorithm confusion attack in jws-3.3.js via missing key-type validation. While the jws-algorithm-confusion finding involves signature verification bypass, the root cause (missing HMAC key-type validation) differs from the advisory's root cause (incorrect modular inverse from negative exponents in modPow). The vulnerable code locations (src/jws-3.3.js vs ext/jsbn2.js) and mechanisms are distinct.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-4602 describes negative exponent handling in ext/jsbn2.js causing incorrect modular inverses via modPow
Advisory references commit 5ea1c32bb2aa894b4bd29849839afe4f98728195 which fixes ext/jsbn2.js
No finding folder references ext/jsbn2.js or modPow negative exponent handling
asn1-parsing-dos finding is in src/asn1hex-1.1.js with root cause in malformed length field parsing
jws-algorithm-confusion finding is in src/jws-3.3.js with root cause in missing HMAC key-type validation, not modPow negative exponent handling
